title: What is Machine Learning
text: Machine learning is a field of AI where systems learn patterns from data instead of being explicitly programmed with rules. Common approaches include supervised learning, unsupervised learning, and reinforcement learning.

title: What is a Neural Network
text: A neural network is a computational model made of layers of interconnected nodes (neurons) that learn to map inputs to outputs by adjusting connection weights during training, typically using backpropagation.

title: What is a Vector Database
text: A vector database stores data as high-dimensional numerical vectors (embeddings) and supports fast similarity search, which lets applications retrieve semantically related content instead of relying only on exact keyword matches.

title: What is Retrieval-Augmented Generation
text: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) combines a search step over a knowledge base with a language generation step, so a chatbot's answers are grounded in retrieved, up-to-date information rather than only what the model memorized during training.

title: What is Fine-Tuning
text: Fine-tuning is the process of taking a pretrained model and continuing training on a smaller, task-specific dataset so it adapts to a narrower domain or style while keeping most of its general knowledge.

title: What is Prompt Engineering
text: Prompt engineering is the practice of carefully designing the instructions and examples given to a language model to reliably elicit the desired kind of response, without changing the model's underlying weights.

title: Mixture-of-Experts Models
text: Mixture-of-experts (MoE) architectures activate only a subset of a model's parameters for each input, which lets total model capacity scale up while keeping the compute cost of each forward pass roughly constant.

title: Model Context Protocol
text: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard that lets AI assistants connect to external tools and data sources through a common interface, instead of each integration needing custom, one-off code.

title: Agentic Workflows
text: Agentic AI workflows let a model plan multi-step tasks, call external tools, observe the results, and adjust its plan, rather than producing a single one-shot response to a prompt.

title: Constitutional AI
text: Constitutional AI is a training approach where a model critiques and revises its own outputs against a written set of principles, reducing reliance on large volumes of human-labeled feedback for alignment.

title: Sparse Attention
text: Sparse attention mechanisms restrict each token to attend to only a subset of other tokens rather than the full sequence, allowing transformer models to process much longer inputs at lower computational cost.
